methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $8.6B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $3.8B - $312.5B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Wayve, a Series D AI & Software company founded by Amar Shah and Alex Kendall, has raised $2.5B+ against a current post-money valuation of $8.6B. The valuation assessment draws from a comparable universe of 9 peers spanning public and late-stage private companies in AI & Software, including Palantir (public), Figure AI, Skild AI, Crusoe Energy, and SandboxAQ, among others.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $8.6B Series D post-money and applying a typical 1.5x step-up multiplier to next-stage funding, implies a valuation of $12.9B. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $12.0B, with a range spanning $3.8B (minimum) to $312.5B (maximum)—the latter reflecting Palantir's Series E valuation and wide dispersion in the AI & Software space.

The triangulated analysis across both methods supports a valuation range of $10.2B (low) to $14.8B (high), with a median of $12.4B. This implies modest step-up from the current $8.6B valuation. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion within the peer set, where valuations vary by two orders of magnitude, and the limited maturity of comparable Series D AI companies. Stage-progression and peer-median results align closely at $12.9B and $12.0B respectively, lending moderate confidence to the $12–13B midpoint range.
